critic_raw:
This design evaluates a CNN predicting echocardiogram-derived heart disease risk scores against a linear regression baseline using tabular clinical features, framed as a regression task.

The design specifies that after hyperparameter selection on the validation set, the CNN is retrained on the combined train+validation data before final test evaluation. This practice introduces a subtle but real threat to validity: the hyperparameters were selected to minimize validation MAE on a specific held-out fold. Once the model is retrained on train+val combined, the regularization strength, dropout, and early stopping patience no longer apply to the same optimization problem — the training set is larger, the noise structure is different, and the optimal stopping point may differ. The validation performance that justified the hyperparameter selection no longer applies to the model actually being tested. This systematically overstates the reliability of the model selection procedure.

The label is a continuous heart disease risk score (0–100) derived from standardized echocardiographic measures including ejection fraction, MAGGIC score components, and similar. The CNN's target is a derived composite score, not a direct clinical outcome. The construction of this score from ejection fraction and other imaging-derived measures means the CNN may be partially regressing on features that can themselves be extracted from the echo images — creating a situation where the CNN could learn to predict the label by learning to extract the same imaging features the cardiologists used to construct the label. This is not explicit leakage, but it inflates the apparent information gain from images because the label's information content is not independent of the image content.

ImageNet pretraining for echocardiogram sequences is a common but contested choice. ImageNet features are tuned to photographic RGB images with natural scene statistics. Echocardiograms are grayscale ultrasound images with speckle noise, dynamic range properties, and structure entirely unlike natural scenes. The pretrained features may be counterproductive, and fine-tuning from these initializations may require substantially more data to overcome the prior. The design uses ResNet-18 or DenseNet-121 but does not include a randomly-initialized control, so whether ImageNet initialization helps or hurts the echocardiogram task is untested.
all_issues_raised: Retraining the CNN on combined train+validation data after hyperparameter selection on validation alone means the selected hyperparameters no longer apply to the model being evaluated — the validation-optimized configuration is tested on a differently trained model.

The risk score label is derived from echocardiographic measurements including ejection fraction and other imaging features, so the CNN may partly learn to predict the label by extracting the same image features that define it — inflating apparent image-based prediction gains.

ImageNet pretraining for echocardiogram images is an unvalidated assumption: ultrasound images have fundamentally different statistical properties than natural photographs, and no randomly-initialized baseline is included to test whether ImageNet initialization helps or hurts.
all_issues_adjudicated: Retraining the CNN on combined train+validation data after hyperparameter selection on validation alone means the selected hyperparameters no longer apply to the model being evaluated — the validation-optimized configuration is tested on a differently trained model.

The risk score label is derived from echocardiographic measurements including ejection fraction and other imaging features, so the CNN may partly learn to predict the label by extracting the same image features that define it — inflating apparent image-based prediction gains.

ImageNet pretraining for echocardiogram images is an unvalidated assumption: ultrasound images have fundamentally different statistical properties than natural photographs, and no randomly-initialized baseline is included to test whether ImageNet initialization helps or hurts.
